summary: AI opponent behavior system: main update loop (ai_update), reaction table lookup, action sequence parser, 16 tactic functions (sAiTacticTable[]), difficulty 0-7 scaling, distance management, guRandom PRNG.

Core AI engine: ai_move_force — Reset AI + force specific move execution
ai_update — Main per-frame AI update (decision loop)
ai_state_reset — Reset all AI action state (clear pointers/flags)
ai_advance_action — Advance to next action in action sequence
ai_execute_action — Execute current action from sequence
ai_difficulty_increase — Increment difficulty + repopulate reaction tables
ai_tables_clear — Clear all reaction tables to difficulty 0
ai_reaction_lookup — Look up reaction by enemy action + char config
ai_enemy_action_check — Check enemy's current action for reaction
ai_self_action_reaction — Check reaction triggered by self action
ai_select_action_full — Main selector: candidate search → filter → random pick
ai_random_chance — Random success check vs difficulty
Tactic functions (sAiTacticTable[16]): ai_tactic_guard_far — Guard when enemy is far + past mid-anim
ai_tactic_approach — Approach enemy when close + past mid-anim
ai_tactic_mid_guard — Guard in first half of animation only
ai_tactic_fail — Always fail (-1) — force sequence advance
ai_tactic_delay_reset — Reset delay timer + set timer callback
ai_tactic_rewind — Rewind action sequence pointer by 2
Extra callbacks (not in array): ai_tactic_timer — Countdown timer callback (1 while >0, -1 when done)
ai_tactic_passive — Passive delay (retreat if enemy action found)
ai_tactic_special_delay — Special move delay (wait for special flag)

globals: sAiRandDelayTable[8] — Random delay jitter by difficulty
sAiAttemptCapTable[8] — Max attack attempts before AI backs off
sAiTimerBaseTable[8] — Timer base value by difficulty
sAiOpenWindowTable[12] — Opponent open window by char ID
sAiCloseWindowTable[18] — Opponent close window by char ID
types: typedef struct { s16 index; x_eea787ea *entry; } AiCandidate
subsystems: Reaction tables: maps enemy actions → response via x_acda7e96[]
Difficulty: rounds 0-7 control cooldowns, patience, aggression
Distance: inter-fighter distance vs character-specific thresholds

sAiTacticTable[16]: guard, approach, reject, delay, mid_guard, aggressive, fail, retreat, special_decide, aggressive_decide, delay_reset, guard, wait_interrupt(x2), check_reaction, rewind + extra callbacks
open_questions: What controls the sAiDelayTable[10] having 10 entries vs 8 for other tables?
